_comment: Trigger predicates for the random-event lifecycle (§9). Each entry names the `event_id` it fires (in data/events.json) and a conjunction of optional predicates, ALL of which must hold for the player being scanned: min_turn/max_turn (a turn window; min_turn is scaled by the game pace's growth_scale, so timers stretch on slower speeds), tech_required (player holds the tech), building_required (player owns the structure in any city), terrain_required (a player city sits on that terrain), at_war / at_peace (player's war state). `probability` (default 100) is the percent chance the armed trigger fires on an eligible turn; `weight` breaks ties when several triggers arm on the same turn (one event fires per player per turn). `one_shot` true fires at most once per player (tracked in Player.events_fired by trigger id). Only sub-100 probabilities and multi-armed turns draw gs.rng, so a lone certain trigger fires without perturbing the stream.
